full_text:
October 21, 2024Professor Alfonso Valencia, Professor Janet Kelso, Professor Inanc BirolExecutive EditorsJournal ofBioinformaticsDear Editor,On behalf of all authors, I am pleased to submit our manuscript entitled“Tribus: Semi-automated disco-very of cell identities and phenotypes from multiplexed imaging and proteomic data”to Journal ofBioinformaticsfor consideration the possibility of its publication.Multiplexed imaging and proteomic technologies allow researchers to quantify and monitor cellular ac-tivity at single-cell resolution levels. However, cell-type annotation has become a critical and challengingtask due to e.g. complex marker combinations, batch effects and artifacts, and the labor-intensive natureof manual gating.In this manuscript, we presentTribus, a knowledge-based classiﬁer designed to perform cell-type anno-tation in multiplexed imaging and proteomic datasets. Tribus uses the logic table as prior knowledge forthe classiﬁcation, and can solve the challenges by:• Allowing ﬂexible adjustment of markers and target cell types.• Apply the logic table per sample iteratively.• Overcoming the need for the labor-intensive setting of thresholds per marker channel.We exhaustively benchmark Tribus with similar methods on four public datasets with ground-truth la-bels. Tribus signiﬁcantly outperforms the other methods in terms of efﬁciency and accuracy.

ively benchmark Tribus with similar methods on four public datasets with ground-truth la-bels. Tribus signiﬁcantly outperforms the other methods in terms of efﬁciency and accuracy. Importantly,Tribus can recover ﬁne-grained cell types as accurately as human experts while allowing the discovery ofphenotypically distinct cell subtypes. Finally, we showcase the usability of Tribus on two large in-housewhole slide image datasets. Further, to ensure smooth integration of Tribus with different image-analysisworkﬂows, we have developed a user-friendly Napari plug-in for Tribus enabling fast visualization andinterpretation of the results.We believe that our manuscript will be of great interest to the readers of Brieﬁngs in Bioinformatics, asit reports a novel tool to solve the critical needs in the analysis of large-scale multiplexed imaging data.We conﬁrm that the data in this manuscript has not been submitted to, nor published in any otherprimary scientiﬁc journal and that all authors are aware of, and agree with, the manuscript’s contents.We greatly appreciate your time in consideration of our submission.Sincerely yours,Anniina F¨arkkil¨a, MD PhDAssistant Professor in Translational Gynecologic OncologyEmail: anniina.farkkila@helsinki.ﬁResearch Program in Systems Oncology & Department of Obstetrics and Gynecology00014 University of Helsinki+358505967786
